description: Confounder: The model assumes that rms_norm_no_affine always forces scale and shift to have

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to both scale and shift, forcing their RMS to 1 in the input dtype (e.g., bfloat16), which affects the magnitude of the modulation even if the conditioning embedding is constant.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes that rms_norm_no_affine always forces scale and shift to have RMS 1, so even with zero conditioning, the modulation should behave consistently. However, when emb is all zeros and lipschitz=True, the linear layer has no bias (bias=False), so scale and shift are exactly zero before normalization; rms_norm_no_affine then divides by zero RMS (after eps), yielding NaNs which become zeros in bfloat16 due to underflow, breaking the expected modulation magnitude.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When lipschitz=True, AdaLayerNormContinuous applies rms_norm_no_affine to both scale and shift, forcing their RMS to 1 in the input dtype (e.g., bfloat16), which affects the magnitude of the modulation even if the conditioning embedding is constant.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
